meta.note: Taiwan + South Korea + Thailand sweep (published books). Deduped against existing TW/KR/TH records and the wider DB — all 29 are new books. Held: Thailand 'Truth on Trial in Thailand' (only a bookseller blurb), South Korea 'With the Century' (borderline DPRK state memoir). Taiwan = KMT martial-law/White Terror bans (lifted 1987 -> historical); sources NMTL virtual museum + Taipei Times. South Korea = 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban (Global Voices) + obscenity/National Security Act cases. Thailand = lèse-majesté / Article 112 and political bans.

entries.title: Rama X: The Thai Monarchy under King Vajiralongkorn
entries.description_ban: Edited Yale SE Asia Studies monograph on King Vajiralongkorn. A Royal Thai Police order of 19 June 2023 (Royal Gazette 7 July 2023) banned its import under the Printing Recordation Act; importing/distributing carries up to 3 years' jail.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific import ban via police order/Royal Gazette; documented by 112Watch and VOA.

entries.title: A Kingdom in Crisis
entries.description_ban: National police chief banned the import/distribution of this book on Thailand's royal-succession crisis on 11 November 2014 (Royal Gazette, 2007 Printing Act), citing content said to defame the monarchy.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific national ban via Royal Gazette; documented by Prachatai and Bangkok Post.

entries.title: Anna and the King of Siam
entries.description_ban: Semi-fictionalised 1944 novel; the book and its film/musical adaptations are banned in Thailand for portraying King Mongkut (Rama IV) in a way deemed insulting to the monarchy. Exact order date not retraced.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Long-standing title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence; no Gazette date).
entries.source_name: Wikipedia (Anna and the King of Siam)

entries.title: Unmasking Thai Society
entries.description_ban: Sulak was charged with lèse-majesté in 1984 in connection with this book and it was suppressed; charges were later dropped under international and royal pressure.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title tied to an Article 112 prosecution and suppression; documented by the Right Livelihood Foundation (medium confidence).

entries.title: Bangkok Inside Out
entries.description_ban: Bangkok guidebook banned by the Ministry of Culture for content on scams, counterfeit goods and prostitution said to tarnish Thailand's image.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Ministry of Culture ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: The True Story of Ah Q
entries.description_ban: Banned in Taiwan after the imposition of martial law as the work of a leftist intellectual who remained in China after 1949.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific White Terror ban; named in the National Museum of Taiwan Literature exhibition.

entries.title: A Brief History of Chinese Fiction
entries.description_ban: Banned in Taiwan with Lu Xun's other works for his left-wing reputation and criticism of the Nationalist government.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific White Terror ban; named in the NMTL exhibition.

entries.title: Rickshaw Boy
entries.description_ban: Banned in Taiwan as the work of an intellectual who remained in Communist China after 1949.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific White Terror ban; documented by the Taipei Times.

entries.title: Spring
entries.description_ban: Volume two of Ba Jin's Torrents Trilogy; banned in Taiwan as the work of a CCP-aligned left-wing writer (named specifically in the NMTL exhibition).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific White Terror ban; named in the NMTL exhibition.

entries.title: Short Novels by Mao Dun, Vol. 2
entries.description_ban: Banned in Taiwan because Mao Dun served as a senior cultural official for the PRC and was seen as promoting Communism.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific White Terror ban; named in the NMTL exhibition.

entries.title: The Generals' Clan
entries.description_ban: Story collection banned by the Taiwan Garrison Command for 'inappropriateness'; a red flag in one story was read as alluding to 'red' China. Distinct from Chen's The Last Day of Summer.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Garrison Command ban; named in the NMTL exhibition.

entries.title: The Legend of the Condor Heroes
entries.description_ban: Banned under the KMT anti-wuxia 'Rainstorm Project'; circulated under altered titles and a faked author name to evade the Garrison Command censors.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific martial-law ban; documented by the Taipei Times.

entries.title: The Locked Heart
entries.description_ban: Domestic Taiwanese novel suppressed for challenging traditional sexual mores — a landmark obscenity-driven ban.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by the Taipei Times.

entries.title: A History of Sex
entries.description_ban: Banned in Taiwan as a work deemed to challenge traditional sexual mores.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by the Taipei Times.

entries.title: Taiwan's 400 Year History
entries.description_ban: Banned for its pro-independence content touching on the 228 Incident; circulated underground, and possession was cited in the 1991 Taiwan Independence Association Case.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia and the Taipei Times.

entries.title: The History of Taiwanese Animals
entries.description_ban: Banned not for its content but because the author was the husband of dissident politician Wong Chin-chu — an example of guilt-by-association censorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by the Taipei Times.

entries.title: Monologue Under Tradition
entries.description_ban: Li Ao's provocative debut; promptly banned and emblematic of the roughly 96 of his books prohibited under martial law.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban within Li Ao's heavily-banned oeuvre; documented via SCMP and academic sources (medium confidence).

entries.title: North Korea's Missile Strategy
entries.description_ban: On the 2008 Ministry of National Defense list of books barred from military barracks as 'pro-North Korea'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: North Korea's Native Culture
entries.description_ban: On the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'pro-North Korea' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: History Has Never Led Me Astray
entries.description_ban: On the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'pro-North Korea' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: Why the 80 Percent Are Controlled by the 20 Percent
entries.description_ban: On the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'pro-North Korea' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: Companion
entries.description_ban: North Korean novel ('Friend'); on the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'pro-North Korea' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: Crimes by US Troops in Korea and the ROK-US SOFA
authors: National Campaign for Eradication of Crimes by US Troops in Korea
entries.description_ban: On the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'anti-American/anti-government' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: Salt-flower Tree
entries.description_ban: Labour-activist essays; on the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'anti-government' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: Blood Falls from the Flowers
entries.description_ban: Collected poems of dissident poet Kim Nam-ju; on the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'anti-government' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: History of the Republic of Korea
entries.description_ban: Revisionist popular history; on the 2008 Ministry of National Defense barracks ban list, 'anti-government' category.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title on the 2008 MND barracks ban list; documented by Global Voices (medium confidence).

entries.title: Happy Sara
entries.description_ban: Novel banned in 1992 for obscenity; the author was arrested mid-lecture and given a suspended sentence, upheld by the Supreme Court in 1995.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific obscenity ban and conviction; documented via Wikipedia and SCMP.

entries.title: The Origins of the Korean War
entries.description_ban: Banned under Chun Doo-hwan; circulated as an underground publication, and Cumings was denied a visa for roughly 15 years.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented in an Asia-Pacific Journal interview with Cumings.

entries.title: The Logic of the Transition Era
entries.description_ban: Top-ranked 'dangerous ideological' book under Park Chung-hee's Yushin regime; the author was prosecuted under anti-communist law and jailed about two years over this and related titles.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia and Korean press (medium confidence).
